title: KAL Action Definition
description: Declarative specification for a single Known Action Library (KAL) action. See /AI One/kal-dsl-spec.md for the narrative spec. Enforced by app/services/kal_registry.py at backend startup; any YAML file under kal/actions/**/*.yaml that fails validation causes the backend to refuse to start (loud + early).
$comment: Sprint D / Task #3 added the `transport`, `cred_type`, and `device_executors` fields to support network-device actions (SSH/NETCONF/SNMP). When `transport` is absent or 'shell', actions retain the original OS-keyed `executors` shape; when `transport` is anything else, the action MUST declare `cred_type` + a vendor-keyed `device_executors` block instead.

id.description: Unique action id, snake_case, matches the YAML filename stem.

version.description: Monotonically-increasing version. Bump on any schema-affecting change. Old versions kept for N months so mid-rollout agents on older binaries still match.

tier.description: Risk tier per KAL v1 spec. 1=autonomous idempotent, 2=approval-required, 3=escalation-only, 4=human-only, 5=monitoring/read-only.

category.description: Slash-separated hierarchy for UI grouping + action discovery. Match the filesystem path under kal/actions/.

description.description: Human-readable description shown in admin UI and used by the AI orchestrator for action selection. Keep ≤ 500 chars; elaborate in kal-dsl-spec.md if needed.

implementation.description: "dsl" = this YAML is the complete implementation. "curated" = stub that points at a hand-coded Go function (Tier 3/4).

transport.description: Selects the executor dispatch path. 'shell' (default) = local fork+exec on the agent host (uses `executors`, OS-keyed). 'ssh' = SSH session against a network device (uses `device_executors`, vendor-keyed). 'netconf' / 'snmp' / 'cloud_api' reserved for future transports.

cred_type.description: REQUIRED when transport != 'shell'. Names the credential kind the agent fetches per action via POST /v1/credentials/issue. Maps directly to the cred_type enum in app/schemas/credentials.py.

patent_claim_refs.description: Patent claim identifiers this action provides RTP evidence for. Example: ["4e", "1-vii"].

deprecated.description: When true, dispatchers refuse new invocations of this action. Existing in-flight executions complete normally.

replaced_by.description: Points at the successor action id. Only meaningful when deprecated=true.

parameters.description: Action parameters + validation. Parameters flow from the AI orchestrator to the executor.

schema.description: JSON Schema draft-07 for the parameter object. `additionalProperties: false` is REQUIRED on parameterful actions (enforced by loader rule #6a).

idempotent.description: Declared, not assumed. Tier-1 actions MUST be idempotent (enforced by loader rule #3). Tier-2+ may declare false but then MUST declare rollback.possible=true.

supported_platforms.description: Platform matrix — which OS+arch combinations this action supports. Agent OS/arch is verified at dispatch; mismatched dispatch returns ErrUnsupportedOS.

executors.description: Per-OS executor specs (transport='shell'). REQUIRED when implementation='dsl' AND transport='shell' (or absent). FORBIDDEN when implementation='curated' OR transport != 'shell'. Keys must match os values in supported_platforms (loader rule #7).

device_executors.description: Per-vendor executor specs for non-shell transports (ssh/netconf/snmp/cloud_api). REQUIRED when implementation='dsl' AND transport != 'shell'. Keys are normalized vendor slugs; values describe the vendor-specific command set or RPC payload.

curated_ref.description: Reference to a hand-coded Go function. REQUIRED when implementation='curated'; FORBIDDEN when implementation='dsl'. Must resolve against the Go-side init()-registered function table (enforced by loader rule #8).

parameter_transforms.description: Derived parameters computed from declared parameters. Enables e.g. Windows timeout-in-milliseconds from user's timeout-in-seconds param without the parameter schema leaking that complexity.

from.description: Source parameter name (must exist in parameters.schema.properties).

by.description: Numeric operand for multiply/divide/add/subtract. REQUIRED for those ops.

timeout_seconds.description: Hard timeout enforced by context.WithTimeout. Exceeding returns ErrTimeout; dispatcher reports status=timed_out.

stdout_regex.description: Optional regex that must match stdout for success. Null disables.

stderr_regex_not.description: Optional regex that MUST NOT match stderr. If it matches, action is failed. Null disables.

state_capture.description: Declarative state-capture wrapper. Each of `pre` and `post` may be either a sentinel string ("none" / "stateless" / "snapshot_*") OR a structured object describing how the agent should collect state on the device. Structured form (Sprint follow-up S2.a) drives the persistent-shell `shellCollector`: the listed commands run in the same shell the action uses, captured output is fed through the named `parser`, and the parser's map is the row's state_payload. `invariants` (optional) is a list of human-readable rules the backend's state_capture_validator_service consults — its DSL is intentionally underspecified here so the validator can evolve without YAML migrations.

oneOf.description: Sentinel form — "none" / "stateless" / "snapshot_<id>". Read-only actions (e.g. show running-config) typically declare "stateless" so the executor skips capture for that phase.

Structured form — the persistent-shell collector runs `commands` (in the same shell the action uses) and feeds the joined output through `parser`. The parser is looked up in the agent's `internal/capture/parsers` registry; an unknown name fails the action with a clear error rather than producing an empty payload.

invariants.description: Optional list of human-readable invariants checked by the backend validator service against the parsed pre+post state_payloads. Free-form for MVP; a structured DSL may replace strings in a future schema version (additive — string form will continue to validate).

pre_required.description: When true, a failure to collect/parse the pre-capture aborts the action body before any device mutation. Default false preserves the historical posture: pre-failures log + continue, the action runs against an unobserved baseline, and the validator decides 'not_applicable' (no pre row to compare). Set true on actions where running blind is unsafe — e.g. a configuration restore that must know the original config to make the diff meaningful. Bucket A.2 / MEDIUM#9 from the post-S4 code review.

rollback.description: Rollback specification. If possible=false, rationale is required. If possible=true, spec is required (enforced by loader rule #9).

rationale.description: REQUIRED when possible=false. Explains why rollback is unnecessary or impossible.

spec.description: REQUIRED when possible=true. Same shape as a top-level executors/device_executors/curated_ref block; describes how to undo the action. For shell-transport actions use ``executors``; for ssh/netconf/snmp/cloud_api actions use ``device_executors``.

allOf.description: implementation=dsl + transport=shell (or absent) requires executors, forbids curated_ref + device_executors.
implementation=dsl + transport in (ssh, netconf, snmp, cloud_api) requires device_executors + cred_type, forbids executors + curated_ref.
implementation=curated requires curated_ref, forbids executors + device_executors.

Tier-1 actions must declare idempotent=true. Tier-2+ may declare false, but only if rollback.possible=true. Loader rule #3 enforces the tier-1 clause; additional cross-field checks (tier-2+ non-idempotent must have rollback) live in the Python loader since JSON Schema can't express multi-field conditionals cleanly.

device_executor_spec.description: Per-vendor executor for non-shell transports. Carries the vendor-specific command(s) the agent runs over the action's transport. Exactly one of `command` (single) or `commands` (list) must be set.

pre_commands.description: Vendor terminal-setup commands run on connect before the user-visible commands. Output is discarded; failures abort the connection. Examples: 'terminal length 0' (Cisco), 'set cli screen-length 0' (Junos).

command.description: Single command to run on the device. Mutually exclusive with `commands`. Supports {{parameter}} interpolation against declared parameters + parameter_transforms (loader rule #6).

commands.description: Ordered list of commands to run in one session. Mutually exclusive with `command`. Each element supports {{parameter}} interpolation. Output is concatenated with per-command marker lines (see sshclient.RunSequence).

parser_template.description: Optional NTC-Templates / TextFSM template name for parsing the device output into structured data. Reserved for D4 (config diff service); the runtime executor ignores this field today.

executor_spec.description: Per-OS executor invocation. `binary` must be absolute + non-shell; interpolation in args is scoped to declared parameters + parameter_transforms.

binary.description: Absolute path to the binary. Unix: starts with '/'. Windows: starts with drive letter (e.g. 'C:\Windows\System32\...'). Must NOT be a shell (loader rule #5 blocklists sh/bash/cmd/powershell/wsl). Must NOT contain path-traversal ('..').

args.description: Literal strings + {{parameter}} interpolation. Each array element is a separate arg (no shell-concatenation). Never a single string that gets split.

fallbacks.description: Ordered list of alternative binary+args to try ONLY when the primary fails with ErrBinaryMissing. Nonzero exits from the primary do NOT trigger fallback.
